---
title: Elements
description: Overview of Studyflow elements
order: 1
---


The main element of a studyflow diagram is the *Study*, which serves as a container for all other elements:

::: {layout-ncol=1}
![**Study** represents the entire research project, including all the processes from experimental design to deployment and publication. It extends `bpmn:Process` and is the top-level container for all elements and workflows.](../assets/img/elements/study.svg)
:::

Within a *Study* element, you can define various elements. In addition to the standard BPMN elements, Studyflow introduces specialized elements for research workflows in cognitive sciences in four main categories: *events*, *activities*, *gateways*, and *data*.

## Events


::: {layout-ncol=2}
![**Start Event** defines the beginning of the workflow. It can be conditioned on participants giving informed consent, supporting the ethical and legal requirements.](../assets/img/elements/start_event.svg)

![**End Event** marks the conclusion of the workflow, indicating the completion of all steps within the study. It can redirect to a different workflow or terminate the study, typically after all the data has been collected.](../assets/img/elements/end_event.svg)
:::



## Activities


::: {layout-ncol=2}
![**Instructions** provide detailed guidelines and information to participants about the study procedures.](../assets/img/elements/instruction.svg)

![**Questionnaires** are used to gather self-reported data from participants.](../assets/img/elements/questionnaire.svg)

![**Cognitive Tests** represent various cognitive assessment tests within the experiment.](../assets/img/elements/cognitive_task.svg)

![**Video Games** are interactive games as part of the experimental paradigm.](../assets/img/elements/video_game.svg)

![**Behaverse Tasks** are standardized cognitive tests covering a range of attentional, perceptual, cognitive and motor abilities. See [Behaverse assessment](https://behaverse.org/projects/cognitive-tests.html) for a list.](../assets/img/elements/behaverse_task.svg)

![**Rest** periods are intervals of inactivity or minimal activity within the workflow, often used for baseline measurements or to provide breaks between activities. This element can also be used to record resting-state signals.](../assets/img/elements/rest.svg)
:::

## Element-to-schema mapping

Each visual element in the modeler maps to a type definition in the Studyflow moddle schemas.

- Event elements map to types such as `StartEvent` and `EndEvent`, then to moddle entries for BPMN event types.
- Activity elements (e.g., `Instruction`, `Questionnaire`, `CognitiveTask`) map to types with domain-specific attributes shown in the inspector.
- Gateway elements map to types like `RandomGateway`, with control-flow attributes encoded in schema properties.
- Data elements map to types such as `Dataset`, `Table`, and `DataCatalog`.


## Gateways


::: {layout-ncol=2}
![**Random Gateway** represents a randomizer at a decision point in the workflow where only one of several possible paths can be taken.](../assets/img/elements/random_gateway.svg)

![Example use of *Random Gateway* to branch a flow.](../assets/img/elements/random_gateway_pattern.svg)
:::


## Data


::: {layout-ncol=2}
![**Data Store** and **Dataset** persist data (e.g., events, trials, etc) and facilitate the integration of standard data models. See [the **Data** reference page](data.qmd) for more details.](../assets/img/elements/dataset.svg)

![**Data Object** represents any data entity within the workflow, such as files, data, or data streams. It can be used to model inputs and outputs of activities and data transformations.](../assets/img/elements/data_object.svg)
:::


The current version studyflow includes built-in support for several data models commonly used in cognitive sciences, including:


::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Behaverse [Events](https://behaverse.org/data-model/spec/events/index.html) data model](../assets/img/elements/bdm_events.svg)

![Behaverse [Trials](https://behaverse.org/data-model/spec/trials/index.html) data model](../assets/img/elements/bdm_trials.svg)

![BDM [Statistics & Models](https://behaverse.org/data-model/spec/#)](../assets/img/elements/bdm_models.svg)

![[Brain Imaging Data Structure](https://bids.neuroimaging.io/)](../assets/img/elements/bids.svg)

![[Psych-DS](https://psych-ds.github.io/) dataset standard](../assets/img/elements/psych-ds.svg)

![[Kedro datasets](https://docs.kedro.org/projects/kedro-datasets/)](../assets/img/elements/dataset.svg)
:::
